verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically anchored via N-myristoylation to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane and is not surface-accessible at baseline. However, in cancer cells, Src undergoes noncanonical topological inversion via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), which translocates the protein to the outer leaflet where it becomes accessible to extracellular binders. This cancer-cell-restricted ecto-pool qualifies as cell-state-induced surface presentation.
